meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no portfolio. Learn how to implement professional analytics with Antigravity and Claude Code in this beginner-friendly developer guide.
content.answer_block: To transform your student project into a portfolio piece, integrate analytics to prove real-world usage. By tracking user events through Antigravity and automating the implementation with Claude Code, you demonstrate data-driven decision-making, a critical skill that turns a simple school assignment into a professional, hireable software engineering case study.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks coding, debugging, and styling, yet your GitHub repository sits in silence. The brutal reality of the tech industry is that code alone doesn't prove impact—data does. Without visibility into how users interact with your application, your project remains a static artifact rather than a living product.

But here is the shift: when you add professional-grade analytics, you transition from a coder to a product-minded engineer. By documenting user journeys and conversion metrics, you provide tangible evidence of your app's value. This guide will show you how to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to transform your project into a data-backed portfolio centerpiece.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Identify the two most critical user actions that define success for your app. Don't track everything; track what matters.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project codebase and suggest 3 high-value user events that would demonstrate product-market fit to a potential employer.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Analyze my project structure and suggest 3 key events to track for a portfolio case study.'

steps.title: Initialize Antigravity Analytics
steps.description: Install the SDK and configure the provider to capture events without bloating your core business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, reusable analytics wrapper for Antigravity that handles event tracking and error logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'npm install antigravity-sdk && npx antigravity init'

steps.title: Instrument Key User Flows
steps.description: Embed tracking calls into your main interaction points like 'sign-up', 'feature-trigger', and 'error-occurrence'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my current component to include an analytics event call upon button click without breaking the existing state management.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit 'Add trackEvent calls to the user flow in src/components/MainAction.tsx'

steps.title: Automate Data Visualization
steps.description: Ensure your dashboard reflects the data you are collecting to make it ready for your portfolio presentation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a SQL query schema for Antigravity that aggregates my tracked events into a monthly active user report.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Generate a summary dashboard configuration for my tracked user events.'

steps.title: Document for Portfolio Impact
steps.description: Transform your raw data into a 'Case Study' README section that highlights your engineering process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a README.md section that explains how I used analytics to optimize my app, focusing on the 'before and after' metrics.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit 'Update README.md to include a Data-Driven Improvements section based on my analytics setup.'

pro_tips: Always use event-naming conventions like 'action_performed' rather than generic names to make your logs readable for recruiters.
Track error rates as a 'negative event'—showing you monitor stability proves you think like a Senior Developer.
Don't expose your API keys; use environment variables and ensure they are excluded from your public GitHub repository.
Add a 'source' property to your events to track which features drive the most engagement during user testing.
Keep your analytics footprint lightweight to ensure your app performance remains high, which is a key metric for hiring managers.
content.next_steps: Now that your tracking is live, deploy your app to Vercel or Netlify, share the link with three peers to generate test data, and then write your 'Case Study' blog post.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just building features; you are building a product that proves your worth to any engineering team. Don't let your code go unnoticed. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
